steps.title: Use the quadratic formula
steps.content:
Rewrite the equation in standard form:
$$
6x^2-11x+c-4=0
$$
The quadratic formula gives:
$$
x=\frac{11\pm\sqrt{(-11)^2-4(6)(c-4)}}{12}
$$
$$
x=\frac{11\pm\sqrt{217-24c}}{12}
$$

steps.title: Match the discriminant
steps.content: The given solution has $\sqrt{601}$ in the numerator, so: $$ 217-24c=601 $$ $$ -24c=384 $$ $$ c=-16 $$ The answer is -16.
